concept: Control / Constrain or Adjust a Technical Process Toward a State
description: To adjust a technical process or variable toward a selected state. The word stays inside the technical fence on purpose: it names an engineer's hand on a dial, never a person commanding another person.
sound_symbolism: A clean velar stop begins 'ke', precise as a fingertip finding a dial. Stress lands on dental 'to', another closure made farther forward and released into round 'o'. Rolling 'ra' carries the adjustment onward and opens its range, while the two measured contacts give the word the feel of correction by small deliberate degrees.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb with the controlling component or process as subject: 'ha monaki phialu selu ketora.' (This component controls the water flow.) The event-noun rule supplies control. Distinct from the causative 'ka', 'kulo' (guide), 'theluo' (steward), and any claim of authority over a person or community.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Transparent control relations let communities examine how infrastructure responds, who selects its target conditions, and who bears unintended effects.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Restricting the unmarked module sense to technical variables prevents a systems metaphor from making domination of people sound neutral or inevitable.

tags.activity: adjustment or constraint of a technical process
tags.cognition: control depends on a selected state or range rather than an unspecified desire
tags.creation: a designed relation used to influence system behavior
